ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn class exists for a double fish burger feast meal set. Closest defensible match is FOODON_00002473 (fish sandwich). The feast format including fries, drink, and pie is not captured by this code.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (fish burger) is the closest defensible code. The full feast set meal format (with fries, drink, pie) is not represented in FoodEx2 at this level of specificity.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for fish burger feast meal set. SNOMED CT coverage for composite fast food meal sets is limited.

serving.description: 1 feast set (burger + medium fries + drink + pie)
serving.natural_language_portion: One feast set comprising a double fish burger, medium fries, a regular soft drink, and a pie, estimated at approximately 750 g total weight based on typical fast food meal set composition.

nutrient_source.title: Spicy Tomato Double Fish Burger Feast — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database record for Spicy Tomato Double Fish Burger Feast (cr_id G00010112). All per-100g values are taken as reported. Saturated fat is not separately reported in the HPB record; value estimated from USDA FoodData Central fish burger analogue (USDA SR Legacy item 21120) and documented below.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific feast set. GI of 72 is estimated using the analogous food 'white bread-based fast food fish sandwich with refined-carbohydrate sides' from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 (Am J Clin Nutr). The burger bun is refined white bread (GI ~70–75), battered fish patty adds refined starch coating, and French fries have GI ~63–75. The composite meal GI is estimated at 72 (high). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × available carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 72 × (29.57 × 0.75 × 10) / 100 ≈ 159.7 for a 750 g feast set (available carbohydrate ~221.8 g). This is a very high glycemic load meal.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium (487 mg/100 g) with low potassium-to-sodium ratio (197 mg K vs 487 mg Na per 100 g); feast format adds further sodium from salted fries and spicy sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this meal set due to its high sodium density (487 mg/100 g) and very high total sodium load across the full feast; replacing the soft drink with water and omitting fries would reduce but not eliminate the sodium concern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI (~72) refined-carbohydrate meal with 29.57 g carbohydrate per 100 g, low dietary fibre (1.8 g/100 g), sugar-sweetened beverage, and deep-fried high-GI French fries creating a very high total glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid this feast set as the combination of a refined white bun, double battered fish patties, deep-fried fries, and a sugar-sweetened soft drink produces a very high glycemic load that will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (487 mg/100 g), estimated saturated fat (~2.8 g/100 g), trans fat (0.1 g/100 g), deep-fried preparation generating oxidised lipids, and sugar-sweetened beverage all contribute to cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal set due to its high sodium, trans fat, saturated fat, and deep-fried lipid oxidation products; the sugar-sweetened soft drink further raises triglyceride and cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) is pro-inflammatory; tomato sauce contains nightshade solanine; soft drink adds refined sugar promoting inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this meal entirely, particularly during flares, as double deep-fried fish patties and fries generate AGEs, trans fat is strongly pro-inflammatory, and the sugar-sweetened drink promotes systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy Tomato Double Fish Burger Feast is a Singapore fast food meal set comprising two battered and deep-fried fish patties layered with spicy tomato sauce on a refined white burger bun, served as a feast set with medium French fries, a regular soft drink, and a pie. The burger patties are coated in a seasoned batter and deep-fried, producing a crispy exterior with a moist fish interior. The spicy tomato sauce adds a tangy, mildly hot flavour profile. The bun is a standard soft white bread roll. The feast format significantly increases the total caloric, carbohydrate, sodium, and sugar load beyond the burger alone. Per 100 g, the meal provides 263 kcal, 13.3 g protein, 10.18 g fat, and 29.57 g carbohydrate with only 1.8 g dietary fibre and 487 mg sodium. The glycemic index is estimated at 72 (high), and the glycemic load for the full feast set is very high. Clinically, this meal is unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high sodium, refined carbohydrate, trans fat, deep-fried lipid oxidation products, and sugar-sweetened beverage components. It is classified as ultra-processed and is a typical Western fast food offering adapted for the Singapore market.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database with confidence 0.95 as reported in the input record. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA SR Legacy fish burger analogue and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021, clearly documented. All other fields are derived from approved sources or documented as unavailable.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (2.8 g/100 g) is not reported in the HPB source record; value estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy item 21120 (fish sandwich, fast food) as the closest approved-source analogue. This is an adapted value. 2. Trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) is referenced from the input health_suitability section; not separately confirmed in HPB per-100g table — treated as an approximate value from the original record's clinical notes. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific Singapore fast food feast set are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 4. Serving size of 750 g is an estimate for the full feast set (burger + medium fries + drink + pie); no official total weight is published by HPB for this set. 5. Glycemic index of 72 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. Halal status assumed based on Singapore fast food market context; not independently verified from a halal certification body. 7. The feast format (fries + SSB + pie) substantially increases the total glycemic load, sodium, and caloric intake beyond the per-100g values for the burger component alone.
